MattermostCustomerFeedbackAnalysisTask:
Analyze the 'customer-feedback' channel. Identify all NEGATIVE feedback items (complaints, bugs, issues). 1. Send a summary email to 'product@company.com' with subject 'Weekly Negative Feedback Digest' listing these items.
2. Schedule a meeting 'Feedback Review' for next Friday at 14:00 in the Calendar.
3. Reply to the channel with 'Feedback logged and meeting scheduled'.
MattermostDeadlineReconciliationTask: I suspect there's a discrepancy between what was discussed and what got documented. Please verify by checking the project-updates channel on Mattermost for any messages mentioning 'deadline' or 'milestone'. For each deadline mentioned, check if there's a corresponding calendar event within ±2 days of the mentioned date. Generate a reconciliation report showing: Deadlines with matching calendar events (PASS), Deadlines WITHOUT calendar events (- Missing), Calendar events not mentioned in chat (📅 Untracked). Email this report to dylan@gmail.com with subject 'Deadline Audit Report'. For each missing calendar event, create it with title '[AUTO] {deadline description}' and post confirmation in the project-updates channel listing the created event titles.
MattermostEmailTask: Sam has sent me a signed contract on Mattermost. Send it to our Legal (legal@company.com) through email. Include the tracking code. Send sam a quick confirmation when you're done. Thanks.
MattermostIncidentEscalationTask:
Monitor the 'support-tickets' channel for CRITICAL incidents. If a critical ticket is found:
1. Create a dedicated channel named 'incident-<ticket_id>' (e.g., incident-ticket-123).
2. Add user 'sam.oneill@neuralforge.ai' to this new channel.
3. Email 'cto@company.com' with subject 'CRITICAL INCIDENT: <ticket_id>' and the issue description.
4. Schedule a 'Discussion on <ticket_id>' meeting for tomorrow at 09:00 AM in the calendar.
MattermostProjectHandoverTask: I'm passing the Phoenix project to Alex. Add Alex to the phoenix channel on mattermost.  ping everyone to schedule a 1-hour project meeting on Monday (find an available time slot from my calendar). Use this exact format in your message: `Meeting Time: [YYYY-MM-DD] from [HH:MM] to [HH:MM]`
MattermostProjectStatusReportTask:
I need a comprehensive project status report. Check these Mattermost channels: 'backend-team', 'frontend-team', and 'qa-team' for status updates. For each team, identify items marked as 'blocked', 'at-risk', or 'on-track'. Cross-reference with calendar milestones to verify if timeline claims are accurate. Generate a risk matrix report in the following format: 
On Track (status=on-track AND milestone exists within 3 days): <item name>, <item name>, ... (and so on for all on-track items) 
At Risk (status=at-risk OR no milestone found): <item name>, <item name>, ... (and so on for all at-risk items) 
Blocked (explicitly marked blocked): <item name>, <item name>, ... (and so on for all blocked items) 
Email this report to 'pm@company.com' with subject 'Sprint Status Risk Matrix'. For any blocked items, create a calendar event titled '[ESCALATION] {item name}' on the next business day at 10:00-10:30. Post a summary in the 'project-sync' channel with the counts: X on-track, Y at-risk, Z blocked.
MattermostReadingGroupTask: Please help me complete the task in mattermost reading group following sam's request.
MattermostResourceConflictResolutionTask:
Check the 'resource-booking' channel on Mattermost for resource requests. Cross-reference each request with the calendar to determine status. Send a report email to 'facilities@company.com' with subject 'Resource Booking Conflicts' in the following format:
APPROVED: <resource>, <resource>, ... (no calendar conflict)
CONFLICT: <resource>, <resource>, ... (overlaps existing booking)
For approved requests, add calendar events titled 'BOOKED: [resource] - [requester]'. For conflicts, DM the requester about the issue.
MattermostShiftCoverageTask: Review shift swap requests in 'shift-requests' channel. Check the Calendar for any conflicts on the requested dates. If there is a conflict (e.g. 'All Hands' or 'Training'), reply to the request in the channel with 'Denied: [Reason]'. If there is no conflict, forward the request via email to 'hr@company.com' with subject 'Shift Swap Request' and reply to the user 'Request escalated to HR'.
MattermostTechnicalDebtTriageTask:
Review the 'tech-debt-review' channel on Mattermost for technical debt discussions. Team members have posted complexity analysis using mathematical formulas. Your tasks:
1. Identify the module with the HIGHEST complexity score (look for the LaTeX formula results).
2. Send Sarah an SMS with the highest-priority module name and its complexity score.
3. Create a new contact named 'Refactoring Team' with phone number '15559876543' and add the company 'TechDebt Solutions Inc'.
4. Post a summary in the 'tech-debt-review' channel listing all modules sorted by complexity (highest first) in a markdown table format.
MattermostVisualInstructionResponseTask:
Check the 'emergency-response' channel on Mattermost. The operations manager has posted a whiteboard photo with emergency contacts and shift times. Please:
1. Create new contacts for the people listed in the 'Emergency Contacts' image. Only fill the first name and phone number.
2. Set alarms for the shift start times listed in the 'Shift Schedule' image, using the shift name as the alarm label.
